create_session.response: 200 OK with uploadSession: { uploadUrl, expirationDateTime, nextExpectedRanges? }
notes: The item name in the body must match the {fileName} path segment.
uploadUrl is preauthenticated; treat it as an opaque absolute URL.

upload_fragment.method: PUT (to uploadUrl, not the Graph base)

headers.Content-Range: bytes {first}-{last}/{total} — total must be identical in every fragment

rules: Do NOT send the Authorization header to the uploadUrl; the session URL is preauthenticated and adding the bearer token can produce 401.
Fragments must be uploaded sequentially, in order.
Every fragment except the last must be a multiple of 320 KiB (327,680 bytes); each request must stay under 60 MiB.
202 Accepted with { expirationDateTime, nextExpectedRanges: ["{next}-"] } while ranges remain.
The final fragment returns 200 or 201 with the completed driveItem (id, name, size, file).

notes: nextExpectedRanges entries are zero-indexed "start-end" or open-ended "start-" strings; resume from the start of the first missing range.
nextExpectedRanges does not always list every missing range and is not a template for fragment sizes.

error_recovery.416: Requested Range Not Satisfiable — the server already has those bytes. Not fatal: query upload status and resume from the first missing range.
error_recovery.5xx: Retry the same fragment after a backoff delay (injected; 1, 2, 4, ... seconds per consecutive failure), up to max_retries consecutive failures, then fail.
error_recovery.404: The upload session expired or was discarded. Recovery is to create a brand-new session and re-upload from byte 0; a second 404 is terminal.
